join_order.notes: Materialize a filtered adsh_id->sub_row direct lookup once, then scan num and accumulate into sub_row totals before final SQL grouping.

filters.predicate: n.adsh = s.adsh AND s.fy = 2022 after n.uom = 'USD'

data_structures.dim_filter: direct_array(shared adsh id -> filtered sub_row or kEmpty32), built from sub/indexes/adsh_to_rowid.bin and fy=2022
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local direct_array(sub_row -> partial sum) reduced to one dense sub_row sum array, then compact_hash_map(cik->sum) and compact_hash_map(name_id,cik->sum) over filtered sub rows only

optimization_notes: Root cause: Category A plus D. The hot path spends nearly all time in a single-threaded scan that performs random hash aggregation for every qualifying num row even though the join target is a tiny one-row-per-adsh dimension. Change the plan to build a filtered dense adsh_id->sub_row lookup for fy=2022, scan num in parallel with zone-map pruning, and accumulate values into thread-local dense sub_row sum arrays. The dense array is about 86135 * 8 bytes per thread (~689 KB), so even at 64 threads the replicated working set stays around 44 MB and fits inside the 88 MB LLC; the final SQL groupings and AVG threshold are then computed from the much smaller filtered sub-row result set instead of from the full fact stream.
